paper_title: Inferred Title from Methods and Study Area Sections

methods.description: Process for collecting and preserving macrozoobenthos samples from streams to analyze species richness and density.

steps.description: Samples were collected at monthly intervals for one year from November 2007 to December 2008, in the Bzura, Mroga and Mrożyca Streams.
steps.searchable_summary: collect monthly stream samples over one year

steps.description: Ten samples were collected with a 25 cm2 (250 cm2 of stream-bed area) tubular sampler at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: use tubular sampler to collect stream-bed samples

steps.description: Invertebrates from benthic samples were sorted by hand and preserved in 10% formalin prepared with stream water.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and preserve invertebrates in formalin

steps.description: Chironomidae larvae were identified to species level (if possible) or from imagines obtained at laboratory rearing.
steps.searchable_summary: identify Chironomidae larvae to species level

methods.description: Estimation of the impact of riverine abiotic variables on species richness and density of macrozoobenthos.

steps.description: The impact of the following riverine abiotic variables on species richness and density of macrozoobenthos were estimated: discharge, current velocity, width, depth, water temperature, dissolved oxygen and pH.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate impact of abiotic variables on macrozoobenthos

methods.method_name: Particulate Organic Matter and Substrate Analysis
methods.description: Determination of benthic and transported particulate organic matter and analysis of inorganic substrate granulometry.

steps.description: On the basis of these benthic samples we also determined BPOM (amount of two fractions benthic particulate organic matter, BCPOM, coarse-and BFPOM, fine POM, Petersen et al. 1989), TPOM (transported particulate organic matter), as well as periphyton (chlorophyll a concentration, Golterman et al. 1978).
steps.searchable_summary: determine BPOM, TPOM, and periphyton from benthic samples

steps.description: We also estimated the granulometrity of inorganic substrate; SI is a single inorganic substrate index, obtained by summing the mid point values of size classes weighted by their proportional cover (Quinn & Hickey 1990).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate inorganic substrate index from size classes

methods.description: Statistical analysis of environmental variables and benthic groups, including diversity indices and multivariate techniques.

steps.description: Data were log transformed (x + 1), when necessary, to satisfy the requirement of normality and homogeneity of variance (Levene's test).
steps.searchable_summary: log transform data for normality and variance homogeneity

steps.description: One-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was used to examine the temporal variance of the environmental variables and of the dominant benthic groups.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA on environmental and benthic data

steps.description: We also estimated the chironomid diversity (Shannon-Wiener index).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Shannon-Wiener index for chironomid diversity

steps.description: Among multivariate techniques we used hierarchical clustering (CLUSTER) and nonmetric multidimensional scaling (NMS) to determine spatial variation between investigated sites based on chironomid assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CLUSTER and NMS for spatial variation analysis
